title: Bagmati Province - The Kathmandu Post
summary: Sindhupalchok steps up forest fire prevention with fire lines in over 30 community forests By Anish Tiwari District forest office accelerates preparedness as rising heat and wind increase wildfire risks; 82 hectares already gutted. BP Highway blocked after rain washes away diversions in Kavre By Jyoti Shrestha Traffic halted since 11pm Saturday after multiple diversion points along the Katunje–Man
content:
Sindhupalchok steps up forest fire prevention with fire lines in over 30 community forests By Anish Tiwari District forest office accelerates preparedness as rising heat and wind increase wildfire risks; 82 hectares already gutted. BP Highway blocked after rain washes away diversions in Kavre By Jyoti Shrestha Traffic halted since 11pm Saturday after multiple diversion points along the Katunje–Mangaltar stretch were swept away, with vehicles stranded at several locations. Bagmati revises public transport fares after fuel price hike By Pratap Bista Minimum fare in Kathmandu Valley rises to Rs24; taxi and freight rates also increased. Bagmati Province finally gets land for administrative complex By Pratap Bista Lack of a consolidated headquarters for the province has led to high rental costs and logistical delays, reports have pointed out. Mustang Helicopter goes missing in Dolakha after Lapcha take-off By Post Report Aircraft carrying five on board loses contact amid heavy snowfall; search operation underway. Two generations in a makeshift home under a boulder By Poshnath Adhikari A Chepang family in Dhading has been living in a tiny, natural shelter, enduring elements, and foraging wild tubers. Landslide blocks Kanti Highway By Pratap Bista Road section at Gangate in Bhimphedi blocked after rain-triggered landslide; traffic halted in both directions. Rabi Lamichhane commanding lead in Chitwan-2 By Ramesh Kumar Paudel Lamichhane leads by over 27,000 votes as counting nears completion. RSP leads in Sindhupalchok-1, Congress ahead in Sindhupalchok-2 By Ajit Tiwari Early counting shows Bharat Parajuli (RSP) and Bansha Lal Tamang (Congress) in front as votes continue to be tallied. RSP’s Ashika Tamang maintains lead in Dhading-1 By Post Report UML, Congress and NCP candidates trail as early vote count puts RSP ahead. Lamichhane doubles lead in Chitwan-2 as counting progresses By Ramesh Kumar Paudel Rastriya Swatantra Party chief far ahead of rivals in early tally. Rabi Lamichhane takes commanding lead in Chitwan-2 By Post Report Rastriya Swatantra Party chief ahead of UML, NCP, and Congress rivals in early count. Rastriya Swatantra Party’s Sobita Gautam leads in Chitwan-3 By Post Report Gautam ahead of Nepali Communist Party candidate Renu Dahal in early vote count. Voters queue up before dawn in Chitwan By Ramesh Kumar Paudel Polling to elect House of Representatives members will begin at 7am across 162 polling centres in the district. Kavrepalanchok restricts trucks and tippers ahead of elections By Jyoti Shrestha Ban aims to ensure heightened security during elections. 134 inmates who fled Bhimphedi prison still at large By Pratap Bista 26 Indian nationals among fugitives; search intensified, police say. Bus crash in Makawanpur injures 22 By Pratap Bista Passengers taken to Chitwan for treatment. Bankariya community in Makawanpur fears eviction with lease set to expire By Pratap Bista Parsa National Park officials have reportedly urged forest authorities not to renew the land arrangement. Tensions flare in Banepa after tipper kills scooter rider By Jyoti Shrestha Vehicle torched; police fire tear gas to take situation under control. Bus services suspended after families block Araniko transport in Ramechhap By Nawaraj Shrestha Following a deadly Tuesday crash in Manthali, victims’ relatives assaulted drivers and halted buses, prompting the company to suspend services.
